evals.prompt: Please clean up this existing bash script for quoting and portability issues.
evals.expected_output: An active-mode conventions response focused on shell portability and safety.
assertions: Agent treats shell-file editing as an active auto-invoke case.
Agent applies shell portability, shebang, quoting, and command-validation conventions.
Agent keeps the work scoped to conventions rather than generating a new script design.

evals.prompt: Review this Makefile for target hygiene, .PHONY usage, and recipe correctness.
evals.expected_output: An active-mode conventions response for Makefile work.
assertions: Agent treats Makefile editing as an active auto-invoke case.
Agent applies Makefile conventions such as .PHONY, task-oriented names, idempotence, and $$ escaping.
Agent does not broaden into automation redesign.

evals.expected_output: A concise summary of active, empty/help, and check behaviors plus trigger boundaries.
assertions: Agent dispatches to the empty-args handler.
Agent summarizes the three public entry paths: active auto-invoke, empty/help, and check.
Agent names the main non-trigger cases such as CI YAML and script generation.
Agent dispatches to check mode based on the check keyword.
Agent reports convention compliance and violations without widening into full rewrites.
Agent follows the verification-only contract.

evals.prompt: Please update this GitHub Actions workflow YAML to speed up CI.
evals.expected_output: A redirect because CI workflow YAML is out of scope.
assertions: Agent recognizes CI workflow YAML as out of scope for shell-conventions.
Agent redirects to devops-engineer instead of applying shell conventions.
Agent does not treat inline shell snippets inside CI YAML as a shell-conventions trigger.

evals.prompt: Create a new deployment shell script that builds, tags, and pushes images.
evals.expected_output: A redirect because script generation is out of scope.
assertions: Agent recognizes new script generation as out of scope for shell-conventions.
Agent redirects to shell-scripter instead of generating the script.
Agent preserves shell-conventions as a conventions-only skill.

evals.prompt: Fix this Python CLI and also clean up one shell command in the docs.
evals.expected_output: A response that avoids treating incidental shell work as the primary task.
assertions: Agent recognizes that shell is incidental rather than the primary task.
Agent does not auto-trigger shell-conventions as the main skill.
Agent keeps mixed-task non-trigger behavior explicit.
